question:

3. Using rechargeable batteries

option_2: Ultimate technology
option_3: Redundant technology
option_4: Obsolete technology
solution_text: Explanation : (B) • Solar energy is an example of ultimate energy because ultimate technology is a technology that signifies the transition of idea/ thoughts into power/ force. It means with this tech, people’s ideas or thought can be immediately transferred into physical force/power which can do some actions. Solar energy certainly helps in innovation.

question:
Which of the following discoveries is/are associated with Einstein? (UPSC Prelims 1984)

1. Theory of relativity

2. Quantum theory

3. Radio activity

4. Rocket propulsion

The correct options are:
option_1: 1 and 2
option_2: 1, 2 and 3
option_3: 1, 2 and 4
option_4: 1 only
solution_text: Explanation : (A) • The theory of relativity was discovered by Sir Albert Einstein himself whereas he contributed immensely in the quantum theory. Einstein’s early contributions to quantum theory include his heuristic suggestion that light behaves as if it is composed of photons, and his exploration of the quantum structure of the mechanical energies of particles embedded in matter. The theory of radioactivity has no mention of Einstein. Similarly, the rocket propulsion system also has no mentioned contribution of Albert Einstein quantum theory.

question: India has decided to acquire.. to match Pakistan’s F-16............. (UPSC Prelims 1984)
option_1: Jaguars
option_2: MIG-29
option_3: Mirage 2000
option_4: Harpoon
solution_text: Explanation : (B) • India had decided to acquire Russian made fighter aircraft Mig-29 to match Pakistan’s F-16.

question: Chips used in integrated circuits in computers are made of............. (UPSC Prelims 1984)
option_1: Mica
option_2: Manganese
option_3: Silicon
option_4: Zinc
solution_text: Explanation : (C) • An integrated circuit or monolithic integrated circuit (also referred to as an IC, a chip, or a microchip) is a set of electronic circuits on one small plate (“chip”) made of semiconductor material, normally silicon. ICs are designed for different purposes. A chip may be designed as a calculator, which only works as a calculator. Integrated circuits can be classified into analog, digital and mixed signal. (both analog and digital on the same chip).

question: India carried out its first underground nuclear explosion at............... (UPSC Prelims 1984)
option_1: Thumba
option_2: Pokhran
option_3: Trombay
option_4: Khemkaran
solution_text: Explanation : (B) • India’s first underground nuclear explosion was at the Pokhran Test Range in Rajasthan on May 18, 1974. • The Code name was Operation Smiling Buddha. • The test was officially called a “peaceful nuclear explosion” (PNE). However, it was a demonstration of India’s nuclear technology and gave it entry into the club of nuclear nations.

question:
State the chronological order in which the following events took place............. (UPSC Prelims 1985)

1. Launching of APPLE

2. Launching of Rohini

3. Antarctica expedition

4. Indian cosmonaut

Codes
option_1: 3-1-2-4
option_2: 2-3-1-4
option_3: 2-1-3-4
option_4: 3-2-1-4
solution_text:
Explanation : (C)

India’s first successful indigenous launch of a Rohini satellite (RS-1) took place on 18 July 1980, followed by the experimental geostationary communication satellite APPLE on 19 June 1981. India’s first Antarctic expedition set out in December 1981, and the first Indian cosmonaut, Rakesh Sharma, flew aboard Soyuz T-11 on 3 April 1984 . So the events occurred in the order Launching of Rohini → Launching of APPLE → Antarctica expedition → Indian cosmonaut. Thus, the correct answer is (C) 2-1-3-4.

question: A balloon filled with air is weighed (W) so that it just floats in water as shown in the figure. When it is further pushed by a short distance in water, it will.............. (UPSC Prelims 1988)
option_1: Sink to the bottom.
option_2: Stay at the depth where it stands submerged.
option_3: Come back to its original position.
option_4: Sink down a little further but will not reach the bottom.
solution_text: When the balloon is just floating, its weight equals the buoyant force — this is neutral equilibrium. Now, if you push it slightly downward, the external pressure of water increases, compressing the air inside the balloon. Because air is compressible, the volume of the balloon decreases, and hence the volume of water displaced becomes less. Buoyant force depends directly on the volume of displaced water. So as the balloon’s volume decreases, the buoyant force decreases, while its weight remains the same. This means the downward force (weight) becomes greater than the upward buoyant force, and there is now a net downward force acting on the balloon. Since this imbalance increases as the balloon goes deeper (because the air compresses even more), the balloon will continue to sink further and further, and ultimately reach the bottom. Thus, the equilibrium is unstable, and the correct answer is (a) Sink to the bottom.

question:
The blood pressure values of four persons are given below : (UPSC Prelims 1993)

(1) Mrs. X: 90/60

(2) Mr. X: 160/120

(3) Mr. Y: 120/80

(4) Mrs. Y: 140/100

Who among the following has normal blood pressure?
option_1: Mrs. X
option_2: Mr. X
option_3: Mrs. Y
option_4: Mr. Y
solution_text: The normal blood pressure for a healthy adult is approximately 120/80 mm Hg, where 120 mm Hg is the systolic pressure (pressure during heart contraction) and 80 mm Hg is the diastolic pressure (pressure during heart relaxation). Looking at the given data — Mrs. X has 90/60 mm Hg, which is below normal, indicating low blood pressure (hypotension). Mr. X has 160/120 mm Hg, which is much higher than normal, indicating high blood pressure (hypertension). Mrs. Y has 140/100 mm Hg, which is also above the normal range, signifying mild hypertension. Thus, Mr. Y is the one with normal blood pressure.

question: Hybridoma technology is a new biotechnological approach for commercial production of............ (UPSC Prelims 2000)
option_1: Monoclonal antibodies
option_2: Interferon
option_3: Antibodies
option_4: Alcohol
solution_text: Hybridoma technology is a biotechnological method developed by Georges Köhler and César Milstein in 1975 for the commercial production of monoclonal antibodies. In this technique, B-lymphocytes (which produce specific antibodies) are fused with myeloma cells (cancerous cells that can divide indefinitely) to form hybrid cells called hybridomas. These hybridoma cells combine the properties of both parent cells — they can grow indefinitely like myeloma cells and produce a single type of antibody like B-cells. The antibodies produced are called monoclonal antibodies, which are identical and specific to a single antigen. They are widely used in diagnosis, treatment of diseases like cancer, and biomedical research. Thus, hybridoma technology is primarily used for the large-scale production of monoclonal antibodies.

question:
With reference to the latest developments in stem cell research, consider the following statements : (UPSC Prelims 2002)

1. The only source of human stem cells are the embryos at blastocyst stage

2. The stem cells can be derived a without causing destruction to blastocysts

3. The stem cells can regenerate themselves in vitro virtually forever

4. Indian research centres also created a few cell lines which can be developed into many types of tissues.

Which of these statements are correct?
option_1: 1, 2, 3 and 4
option_2: 1, 2 and 3
option_3: 1, 2 and 4
option_4: 3 and 4
solution_text: Statement 1 is incorrect because human stem cells are not obtained only from embryos at the blastocyst stage. Apart from embryonic stem cells, adult stem cells (from bone marrow, umbilical cord blood, etc.) and induced pluripotent stem cells (iPSCs), which are created by reprogramming adult cells, are also major sources. Statement 2 is incorrect because the original process of deriving embryonic stem cells involved destruction of the blastocyst, although newer methods can minimize damage — still, it is not completely destruction-free in most practical cases. Statement 3 is correct because stem cells, particularly embryonic and induced pluripotent stem cells, can self-renew and divide indefinitely in vitro under proper laboratory conditions. Statement 4 is also correct because Indian research institutions such as the National Centre for Biological Sciences (NCBS) and the All India Institute of Medical Sciences (AIIMS) have successfully developed human embryonic stem cell lines that can differentiate into various tissue types. Therefore, the correct answer is (D) 3 and 4.

solution_text: (a) Cosmic Background Explorer (COBE) – Satellite programme → Correct. The COBE satellite, launched by NASA in 1989, was designed to measure cosmic microwave background radiation and contributed to Big Bang evidence. (b) Falcon – Undersea cable system → Correct. The FALCON undersea cable system (Fiber-Optic Link Around the Globe) connects countries in the Middle East, Africa, and South Asia, operated by Global Cloud Xchange. (c) Discovery – Space Shuttle → Correct. Space Shuttle Discovery was one of NASA’s operational orbiters, used for many missions including deploying the Hubble Space Telescope. (d) Atlantis – Space Station → Incorrect. Atlantis is not a space station but a space shuttle orbiter of NASA’s fleet, similar to Discovery and Endeavour. Hence, the pair (d) Atlantis – Space Station is not correctly matched.

question: In which one of the following locations is Internationals Thermonuclear Experimental Reactor (ITER) Project to be the built? (UPSC Prelims 2008)
option_1: Northern Spain
option_2: Southern France
option_3: Eastern Germany
option_4: Southern Italy
solution_text: The International Thermonuclear Experimental Reactor (ITER) is a large international nuclear fusion research and engineering project aimed at demonstrating the feasibility of fusion power — the same process that powers the Sun. The ITER project is being built at Cadarache, located in Southern France. It is a collaborative project involving the European Union, United States, Russia, China, Japan, South Korea, and India. The goal of ITER is to produce a self-sustaining fusion reaction where the energy output exceeds the energy input, paving the way for future commercial fusion power plants. The construction began in the mid-2000s, and the site was chosen due to France’s advanced nuclear infrastructure and scientific expertise. Thus, among the given options, Southern France is the correct location for the ITER project.

question: Which one of the following is a spacecraft? (UPSC Prelims 2008)
option_1: Apophis
option_2: Cassini
option_3: Spitzer
option_4: TechSar
solution_text: The Cassini spacecraft, officially known as the Cassini–Huygens mission, was a spacecraft launched jointly by NASA, ESA (European Space Agency), and ASI (Italian Space Agency) in 1997 to study Saturn and its moons. It reached Saturn in 2004 and remained in orbit for over 13 years, providing detailed images and data about Saturn’s rings, atmosphere, and moons — especially Titan and Enceladus. The mission ended in 2017 when Cassini was deliberately plunged into Saturn’s atmosphere to avoid contaminating its moons. Apophis is an asteroid. Spitzer is a space telescope (an observatory). TechSar is an Israeli reconnaissance satellite.

question:
Consider the following : (UPSC Prelims 2011)

1. Photosynthesis

2. Respiration

3. Decay of organic matter

4. Volcanic eruption

Which of the above add carbon dioxide to the carbon cycle on earth?
option_1: 1 and 4 only
option_2: 2 and 3 only
option_3: 2, 3 and 4 only
option_4: 1, 2, 3 and 4
solution_text: Photosynthesis – This process does not add carbon dioxide; instead, it removes CO₂ from the atmosphere. Plants use carbon dioxide, water, and sunlight to produce glucose and oxygen. Hence, it acts as a carbon sink, not a source. 2. Respiration – This process adds CO₂ to the atmosphere. During respiration, both plants and animals break down glucose in the presence of oxygen to release energy, carbon dioxide, and water. 3. Decay of organic matter – When dead plants and animals decompose, microorganisms break down organic materials and release carbon dioxide (and methane) into the atmosphere, thus contributing to the carbon cycle. 4. Volcanic eruptions – Volcanoes release large amounts of carbon dioxide and other gases trapped inside the Earth’s crust into the atmosphere during eruptions. Hence, the processes that add CO₂ to the atmosphere are Respiration, Decay of organic matter, and Volcanic eruption, i.e., statements 2, 3, and 4 only.

question: Aspartame is an artificial sweetener sold in the market. It consist of amino acids and provides calories like other amino acids. Yet, it is used as a low¬calorie sweetening agent in food items. What is the basis of this use? (UPSC Prelims 2011)
option_1: Aspartame is as sweet as table sugar, but unlike table sugar, it is not readily oxidized in human body due to lack of requisite enzymes.
option_2: When aspartame is used in food processing, the sweet taste remains, but it becomes resistant to oxidation.
option_3: Aspartame is as sweet as sugar, but after ingestion into the body, it is converted into metabolites that yield no calories.
option_4: Aspartame is several times sweeter than table sugar, hence food items made with small quantities of aspartame yield fewer calories on oxidation.
